sections.subtitle: Recent Form
sections.text: Benfica has been in exceptional form, remaining unbeaten in their last 11 matches with high-scoring performances (9/10 over 2.5 goals). Sporting Braga, however, is struggling, winless in their last 3 matches, though they've managed to score in most recent games. Benfica's attacking momentum gives them a clear edge.

sections.subtitle: Head-to-Head Record
sections.text: Benfica has dominated recent encounters, winning 4 of the last 5 matches against Braga, including a 1-0 and 3-0 victory in their last two head-to-heads. Braga has failed to keep a clean sheet in 8 consecutive matches against Benfica, indicating a defensive vulnerability.

sections.subtitle: Key Players and Statistics
sections.text: Benfica's Vangelis Pavlidis (18 goals) and Kerem Aktürkoğlu (11 goals, 9 assists) are in top form, posing a significant threat. Braga's Ricardo Horta (10 goals) remains a key asset, but their defensive stats (29 goals conceded) are weaker compared to Benfica's (27 conceded). Benfica's ability to score early (first to score in 9/10 matches) could dictate the game.

sections.subtitle: Season Trends and Tactical Insights
sections.text: Benfica excels in creating big chances and converting them, often scoring in the final minutes (highest % of goals from 76-90 mins). Braga has a decent home record but struggles against top teams. Expect Benfica to control possession and exploit Braga's defensive lapses.
